meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Use SaaS pricing strategies and AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to build, launch, and monetize your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: Project abandonment stems from lack of validation. By implementing a clear SaaS pricing model early, you force market feedback and clarify your value proposition. Use AI-driven scaffolding to build a lightweight MVP, launch a paywall, and pivot based on real revenue data rather than endless feature refinement.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned SaaS projects is filled with founders who chased perfection over profit. You start with a vision, code for weeks, and then drift into the 'feature creep' abyss, losing sight of why you started. The silence from the market isn't a technical failure; it's a validation failure.

The antidote isn't more code—it's revenue. When you attach a price tag to your solution on Day 1, you transform a hobby project into a business. This case study shows you how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build, price, and launch your MVP before your motivation fades.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Pricing
steps.description: Identify the specific pain point you are solving and set a price based on the value delivered, not the hours spent coding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a pricing strategy table for a B2B SaaS tool that solves [INSERT PROBLEM]. Include three tiers: Starter, Professional, and Enterprise, focusing on value-based metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a pricing_config.json file with three tiers based on the generated value-based strategy.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Core
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build only the core functionality required to deliver the value you just priced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal viable codebase for a SaaS that solves [INSERT PROBLEM]. Include user authentication and a Stripe-ready subscription checkout page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/payment_integration.py --description 'Integrate Stripe checkout session logic.'

steps.title: Implement the Paywall Gate
steps.description: Don't hide your pricing. Create a hard gate that requires a subscription to access the core value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks for an active subscription status from the database before allowing access to the main dashboard routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --file src/middleware.py --issue 'Ensure non-paying users are redirected to the pricing page.'

steps.title: Automate Onboarding Flow
steps.description: Reduce friction. Use AI to automate the email sequence that triggers upon successful payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft an automated welcome email sequence for new subscribers that emphasizes the value proposition and guides them to the core feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a new service file for handling post-payment email triggers using a mock SMTP provider.'

steps.title: Launch and Validate
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a live environment and share it with your first potential customers to validate your pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for a production-ready SaaS deployment, including environment variables and database migration scripts.

pro_tips: Always start with a 'Founder's Tier'—manually onboard your first 5 users to gather qualitative feedback.
Never offer a perpetual free tier; offer a 7-day trial to create immediate urgency and qualify leads.
Use your pricing page as a landing page; if nobody clicks 'Buy', the problem isn't the price, it's the messaging.
Leverage Claude Code's memory to keep your technical debt low by refactoring after every successful feature deployment.
If you haven't made a sale in 30 days, kill the project and pivot; the market has spoken.
content.next_steps: After launching your MVP, track your conversion rate from visitor to paid subscriber. If conversion is below 2%, iterate on your pricing tiers or your value proposition using the same AI workflows described above.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. By locking your features behind a price point, you gain the clarity needed to finish what you start. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
